options: 3.1 surfaced as supported version with its own nav group
3.1 folded into 3.2 as migration notes only
entries.question: Should the public 3.x docs expose 3.1 as a separately navigable supported version, or treat 3.1 pages purely as migration notes folded under 3.2?
entries.rationale: Open question explicitly listed in start-here.md; defer until plan phase so the page-classification taxonomy and navigation design address it together.

entries.question: Should the CLI reference be hand-maintained markdown, fully generated from the live Typer tree by a committed script, or hybrid (generated body with hand-authored wrapper prose)?
entries.rationale: Open question explicitly listed in start-here.md; defer until methodology investigation of prior reference commits (a14769e7a, 81b3d6c3e, 514106af2, deee8d7f3) and CLI audit decide whether a generator is justified.

options: All 15 currently-installed harness directories ship as first-class
Tiered: first-class for Claude/Codex/Cursor/OpenCode/Gemini, supported for the rest
Author the matrix first, then promote per-harness pages based on coverage
entries.question: Which AI harnesses ship as officially supported for 3.2 (first-class/supported) vs opportunistically documented (partial/experimental/archived) — and is the per-harness page set the canonical list or only the support matrix?
entries.rationale: Open question explicitly listed in start-here.md; defer until harness research workstream surveys current host integration depth and Spec Kitty's generated files for each.
